comment.body:
@DouweM The current change will not include any `ModelRequest` passed via `message_history` for any runs. So `new_messages()` will only return messages from current run. 
The `run_id` assignment will still happen for `ModelReques`t passed via `message_history` for both `_adapter` and non` _adapter` runs but it wont be returned in `new_messages()`.

Yes this is a behavioral change which was introduced in #4419 and the current change reverts it . Where if a request without a `user_prompt` and no `run_id` was stamped with current `run_id` and included in `new_messages()` assuming since it is a request without a `run_id` it originated from current run and not from a previous run as previous run would have `run_id`s mapped to them.

issue.title: Integrate OpenAI newly `WebSocket` mode in `ResponsesApi`

labels.name: feature
labels.description: New feature request, or PR implementing a feature (enhancement)

issue.body:
### Description

OpenAI just introduced `WebSocket` support for the `ResponsesAPI`, enabling real-time communication with significantly lower latency compared to HTTP requests. This mode maintains a persistent connection where client and server exchange structured events over a single WebSocket session. 
It would be useful to integrate this as an optional mode within the `ResponsesAPI` class, adding connection management and event handling logic to support WebSocket-based interactions alongside the existing HTTP implementation. 


### References
https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/websocket-mode/
_No response_

comment.body: @DouweM got it

issue.title: result.new_messages() always includes user prompt from message_history

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Report that something isn't working, or PR implementing a fix

issue.body:
### Initial Checks

- [x] I'm using the [latest version](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/releases/latest) of Pydantic AI
- [x] I've searched for my issue in [the issue tracker](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/issues) before opening this issue

### Description

After upgrading **pydantic-ai-slim** from **1.63.0** to **1.64.0+**, the behavior of `result.new_messages()` appears to have changed.

When calling `agent.run()` with `message_history` (and without `user_prompt`), the messages supplied in `message_history` used to be treated purely as prior context, so `result.new_messages()` only returned messages generated during the run.

Starting in **1.64.0**, `new_messages()` also returns the user prompt that was provided inside `message_history`.

For example, the following code now returns the original `ModelRequest` as part of `new_messages()` even though it was part of the input history.

This seems related to changes introduced in this PR:
https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/pull/4419/changes#diff-71730070455237accebd709ba7fe41ce60edbd238af02e7d86a208cf8f10ab12R1543-R1545

The last version that behaves as expected for me is **1.63.0**.

Is this an intended change, or a regression? If intentional, is there a recommended way to get only the messages generated during the run?

### Old behavior (≤1.63.0)

`new_messages()` returns only messages generated during the run.

### New behavior (≥1.64.0)

`new_messages()` includes the `ModelRequest` from `message_history`, meaning the user prompt `"hi"` is returned even though it was already part of the provided history.


### Minimal, Reproducible Example

```Python
result = await agent.run(
    message_history=[
        ModelRequest(
            parts=[
                UserPromptPart(
                    content="hi"
                )
            ]
        )
    ],
    deps=deps,
)

print(result.new_messages())
```

### Logfire Trace

_No response_

### Python, Pydantic AI & LLM client version

- Python: 3.12
- Pydantic AI: 1.68.0
- LLM provider SDK: doesn't matter

comment.body:
@DouweM, okay to clear things up 
`ModelRequest` with current `run_id` passed into `message_history` will not be included in `new_messages()` for any run.

The last version that behaves as expected for me is **1.63.0**.

Is this an intended change, or a regression? If intentional, is there a recommended way to get only the messages generated during the run?

issue.title: result.new_messages() always includes user prompt from message_history

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Report that something isn't working, or PR implementing a fix

issue.body:
### Initial Checks

- [x] I'm using the [latest version](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/releases/latest) of Pydantic AI
- [x] I've searched for my issue in [the issue tracker](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/issues) before opening this issue

### Description

After upgrading **pydantic-ai-slim** from **1.63.0** to **1.64.0+**, the behavior of `result.new_messages()` appears to have changed.

When calling `agent.run()` with `message_history` (and without `user_prompt`), the messages supplied in `message_history` used to be treated purely as prior context, so `result.new_messages()` only returned messages generated during the run.

Starting in **1.64.0**, `new_messages()` also returns the user prompt that was provided inside `message_history`.

For example, the following code now returns the original `ModelRequest` as part of `new_messages()` even though it was part of the input history.

This seems related to changes introduced in this PR:
https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/pull/4419/changes#diff-71730070455237accebd709ba7fe41ce60edbd238af02e7d86a208cf8f10ab12R1543-R1545

The last version that behaves as expected for me is **1.63.0**.

Is this an intended change, or a regression? If intentional, is there a recommended way to get only the messages generated during the run?

### Old behavior (≤1.63.0)

`new_messages()` returns only messages generated during the run.

### New behavior (≥1.64.0)

`new_messages()` includes the `ModelRequest` from `message_history`, meaning the user prompt `"hi"` is returned even though it was already part of the provided history.


### Minimal, Reproducible Example

```Python
result = await agent.run(
    message_history=[
        ModelRequest(
            parts=[
                UserPromptPart(
                    content="hi"
                )
            ]
        )
    ],
    deps=deps,
)

print(result.new_messages())
```

### Logfire Trace

_No response_

### Python, Pydantic AI & LLM client version

- Python: 3.12
- Pydantic AI: 1.68.0
- LLM provider SDK: doesn't matter

comment.body:
Hi @orlevii,

The expectation here is that the message history is already stamped with a `run_id` from a previous run. In that case, those messages would not be included in `new_messages()`.

However, if the `message_history` does not have a `run_id` mapped to a message or `ModelRequest` as in your scenario, it will be treated as a new message.

Ideally, if the `message_history` comes from a previous run, each message should already have an associated `run_id`. Having `run_id`s mapped to messages is the most reliable and recommended approach.

@DouweM yes, that can be done.

issue.title: Integrate OpenAI newly `WebSocket` mode in `ResponsesApi`

labels.name: feature
labels.description: New feature request, or PR implementing a feature (enhancement)

issue.body:
### Description

OpenAI just introduced `WebSocket` support for the `ResponsesAPI`, enabling real-time communication with significantly lower latency compared to HTTP requests. This mode maintains a persistent connection where client and server exchange structured events over a single WebSocket session. 
It would be useful to integrate this as an optional mode within the `ResponsesAPI` class, adding connection management and event handling logic to support WebSocket-based interactions alongside the existing HTTP implementation. 


### References
https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/websocket-mode/
_No response_

comment.body:
@DouweM I’m planning to add a WebSocket mode to the existing `OpenAIResponsesModel`. It will be enabled explicitly via `async with model:`. Inside the context, the model will establish a connection with `client.responses.connect()`, and `request()` / `request_stream()` will be routed through the existing Responses stream parser.

HTTP will remain the default transport outside the context. The implementation will fail fast if the context is reused concurrently and will raise clear errors when websocket incompatible settings are used.

issue.body:
Detect pagination elements for multi-page data extraction.

**Location:** `src/heimdall/dom/service.py`

**Tasks:**
- Add `detect_pagination_buttons()` method
- Return structured pagination info

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -63,8 +63,88 @@ async def get_state(self) -> "SerializedDOM":
                 "height": viewport.get("clientHeight", 0),
             }
 
+        # Add pagination detection
+        serialized.pagination_info = self.detect_pagination_buttons(tree)
+
         return serialized
 
+    def detect_pagination_buttons(self, nodes: list["DOMNode"]) -> dict:
+        """
+        Detect pagination elements for multi-page data extraction.
+
+        Returns:
+            Structured pagination info with next, prev, and page buttons.
+        """
+        pagination: dict[str, dict | list[dict] | None] = {
+            "next_button": None,
+            "prev_button": None,
+            "page_buttons": [],
+        }
+        for node in nodes:
+            if not node.is_visible or not node.is_interactive:
+                continue
+
+            text = (
+                (
+                    node.ax_name
+                    or node.attributes.get("aria-label", "")
+                    or node.attributes.get("title", "")
+                )
+                .lower()
+                .strip()
+            )
+
+            rel = node.attributes.get("rel", "").lower()
+            class_name = node.attributes.get("class", "").lower()
+            text_content = text or node.node_name.lower()
+
+            # Simple heuristics for Next
+            is_next = (
+                rel == "next"
+                or "next" in text_content
+                or "»" in text_content
+                or "›" in text_content
+                or ("next" in class_name and ("page" in class_name or "pagination" in class_name))
+            )
+
+            # Simple heuristics for Previous
+            is_prev = (
+                rel == "prev"
+                or "prev" in text_content
+                or "«" in text_content
+                or "‹" in text_content
+                or ("prev" in class_name and ("page" in class_name or "pagination" in class_name))
+            )
+
+            # Detect numeric page buttons or standard texts like "Page 2"
+            is_page_number = False
comment.body: Validated. Numeric pagination detection now bounds values to 1-999 and avoids classifying year-like values (e.g., 2024) as page buttons. Included in commit 813ad80.

- [x] Updated **documentation** for new features and behaviors, including docstrings for API docs.

### Summary

labels.description: Report that something isn't working, or PR implementing a fix

issue.body:
- Closes #4415

### Pre-Review Checklist

- [x] Any **AI generated code** has been reviewed line-by-line by the human PR author, who stands by it.
- [x] No **breaking changes** in accordance with the [version policy](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/blob/main/docs/version-policy.md).
- [x] **Linting and type checking** pass per `make format` and `make typecheck`.
- [x] **PR title** is fit for the [release changelog](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/releases).

### Pre-Merge Checklist

- [x] New **tests** for any fix or new behavior, maintaining 100% coverage.
- [x] Updated **documentation** for new features and behaviors, including docstrings for API docs.

### Summary
- Updated request preparation logic to always set ctx.state.run_id on the trailing processed message when run_id is None (removed the resumed-tail skip case)
- Updated/Added tests to reflect the new behavior (snapshot expectations)

comment.body:
In the snippet above, once is_resuming_without_prompt = True is set, the returned index becomes index of ModelRequest + 1, even though the run_id is being set. For the first request, new_messages still does not include this entry unless the index is recalculated. 
However, I can adjust the _first_new_message_index method to account for that case as well. 

But rather than that i was thinking splitting would be nice option since it makes the flow cleaner. But that would only cover the adapter runs and not the normal runs.
So adding a run_id check could just align with both non-adapter + adapter runs.

Plus now we are mapping run_id to the ModelRequest from message_history let me add some tests too.

issue.title: Fix missing `run_id` on `ModelRequest` in UI adapter runs

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Report that something isn't working, or PR implementing a fix

issue.body:
- Closes #4415

### Pre-Review Checklist

- [x] Any **AI generated code** has been reviewed line-by-line by the human PR author, who stands by it.
- [x] No **breaking changes** in accordance with the [version policy](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/blob/main/docs/version-policy.md).
- [x] **Linting and type checking** pass per `make format` and `make typecheck`.
- [x] **PR title** is fit for the [release changelog](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/releases).

### Pre-Merge Checklist

- [x] New **tests** for any fix or new behavior, maintaining 100% coverage.
- [x] Updated **documentation** for new features and behaviors, including docstrings for API docs.

### Summary
- Updated request preparation logic to always set ctx.state.run_id on the trailing processed message when run_id is None (removed the resumed-tail skip case)
- Updated/Added tests to reflect the new behavior (snapshot expectations)

comment.body:
@DouweM I don't think the above commit will exactly solve the issue.

I think the best way to handle this is to split the user_prompt from message_history before calling run_stream_events, so the _agent_graph’s existing expectations stay intact.

Currently, UI adapters take frontend messages, convert them into ModelMessages, and pass everything including the current user prompt as message_history. The problem is passing in message history is wrong here. As a result, new_messages() misses the request and only returns the response.

What I think would be correct is  before calling the agent, we can split message history and user prompt to the run method. This way the agent_graph does not require changes and this would be better for future updates and is aligned with the expectation defined in the previous commit.

Does this make sense ? This way the flow is easier and the expectations are correct too.

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -532,9 +532,7 @@ async def _prepare_request(
             ctx.state.message_history[:], ctx.deps.history_processors, run_context
         )
         if message_history and message_history[-1].run_id is None:
-            is_resumed_tail = self.is_resuming_without_prompt and _is_same_request(message_history[-1], self.request)
-            if not is_resumed_tail:
-                message_history[-1].run_id = ctx.state.run_id
+            message_history[-1].run_id = ctx.state.run_id
comment.body: done

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -1534,13 +1535,64 @@ def sync_callback(run_result: AgentRunResult[Any]) -> None:
     # Verify we can access messages
     messages = run_result.all_messages()
     assert len(messages) >= 1
+    assert isinstance(messages[0], ModelRequest)
+    assert messages[0].run_id == run_result.run_id
 
     # Verify events were still streamed normally
     assert len(events) > 0
     assert events[0]['type'] == 'RUN_STARTED'
     assert events[-1]['type'] == 'RUN_FINISHED'
 
 
+async def test_adapter_sets_current_run_id_on_trailing_mapped_request() -> None:
+    """The adapter sets `run_id` on the current run's mapped request, not older history."""
+    captured_results: list[AgentRunResult[Any]] = []
+
+    def sync_callback(run_result: AgentRunResult[Any]) -> None:
+        captured_results.append(run_result)
+
+    agent = Agent(TestModel())
+    run_input = create_input(
+        UserMessage(id='msg0', content='Previous question'),
+        AssistantMessage(id='msg1', content='Previous response'),
+        UserMessage(id='msg2', content='Hello!'),
+    )
+
+    await run_and_collect_events(agent, run_input, on_complete=sync_callback)
+
+    assert len(captured_results) == 1
+    run_result = captured_results[0]
+    messages = run_result.all_messages()
+    assert messages == snapshot(
+        [
+            ModelRequest(
+                parts=[UserPromptPart(content='Previous question', timestamp=IsDatetime())],
+            ),
+            ModelResponse(
+                parts=[TextPart(content='Previous response')],
+                timestamp=IsDatetime(),
+            ),
+            ModelRequest(
+                parts=[UserPromptPart(content='Hello!', timestamp=IsDatetime())],
+                timestamp=IsDatetime(),
+                run_id=(run_id := IsSameStr()),
+            ),
+            ModelResponse(
+                parts=[TextPart(content='success (no tool calls)')],
+                usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=IsInt(), output_tokens=IsInt()),
+                model_name='test',
+                timestamp=IsDatetime(),
+                provider_name='test',
+                run_id=run_id,
+            ),
+        ]
+    )
+    assert messages[0].run_id is None
+    assert messages[1].run_id is None
+    assert messages[2].run_id == run_result.run_id
+    assert messages[3].run_id == run_result.run_id

issue.title: `ModelRequest.run_id` empty in `AGUIAdapter` since v1.63

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Report that something isn't working, or PR implementing a fix

issue.body:
### Initial Checks

- [x] I'm using the [latest version](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/releases/latest) of Pydantic AI
- [x] I've searched for my issue in [the issue tracker](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/issues) before opening this issue

### Description

When running an agent in the `AGUIAdapter`, the `AgentRunResult` passed to the `on_complete` handler no longer stores a `run_id` for `ModelRequest`. This seems to be a regression introduced in v1.63, maybe by #4086?

Running agent without adapter:
```
- ModelRequest(parts=[UserPromptPart(content='Hello, agent!', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 2, 24, 9, 47, 44, 980312, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 2, 24, 9, 47, 44, 980447, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='fcd59cb1-271c-4438-998f-6900ac23dbba')
- ModelResponse(...)
```

Running agent with adapter:
```
- ModelRequest(parts=[UserPromptPart(content='Hello, agent!', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 2, 24, 9, 47, 46, 303773, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 2, 24, 9, 47, 46, 305188, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))
- ModelResponse(...)
```

### Minimal, Reproducible Example

```Python
import asyncio

from ag_ui.core import RunAgentInput, UserMessage
from pydantic_ai import Agent, AgentRunResult
from pydantic_ai.ui.ag_ui import AGUIAdapter

agent = Agent(
    name="TestAgent",
    model="openai:gpt-4.1-mini",
)


def on_complete(result: AgentRunResult):
    for message in result.all_messages():
        print(f"- {message}")


async def run_agent():
    result = await agent.run("Hello, agent!")
    on_complete(result)


async def run_agent_with_adapter():
    adapter = AGUIAdapter(
        agent,
        run_input=RunAgentInput(
            thread_id="test-thread",
            run_id="test-run",
            state={},
            messages=[UserMessage(id="msg-1", content="Hello, agent!")],
            tools=[],
            context=[],
            forwarded_props=None,
        ),
    )

    stream = adapter.run_stream(deps=None, on_complete=on_complete)
    async for _ in stream:
        ...


print("Running agent without adapter:")
asyncio.run(run_agent())

print()

print("Running agent with adapter:")
asyncio.run(run_agent_with_adapter())
```

### Logfire Trace

_No response_

### Python, Pydantic AI & LLM client version

- Python: 3.14
- Pydantic AI: 1.63
- LLM provider SDK: OpenAI 2.23.0

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -532,9 +532,7 @@ async def _prepare_request(
             ctx.state.message_history[:], ctx.deps.history_processors, run_context
         )
         if message_history and message_history[-1].run_id is None:
-            is_resumed_tail = self.is_resuming_without_prompt and _is_same_request(message_history[-1], self.request)
-            if not is_resumed_tail:
-                message_history[-1].run_id = ctx.state.run_id
+            message_history[-1].run_id = ctx.state.run_id
comment.body:
This was the expected behavior before #3983. Reverting back to same logic. Ideally messages from previous run passed via  message_history would have a run_id set assuming no changes were done by history processors (i.e rebuilding of messages without run_id). 
In that case this scenario fits well where this logic satisfies both the agent run with _adapter and is_resuming_without_prompt use case well.

comment.body:
@DouweM The expectation was to skip `ModelRequest` if it's from `message_history`. However, I was unaware that adapters use `message_history` even for `UserMessage`. I'll review the current implementation and follow up. Adding a run_id to the final `ModelRequest` in `message_history` (if missing) should resolve this effectively but let me verify once and check for any other ways.

@lukasberbuer Thanks for sharing the flow for persisting the message threads. This makes it easier.

issue.title: fix: Identify new messages by `run_id` instead of index

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Report that something isn't working, or PR implementing a fix

issue.body:
- Closes #3983 

### Pre-Review Checklist

- [x] Any **AI generated code** has been reviewed line-by-line by the human PR author, who stands by it.
- [x] No **breaking changes** in accordance with the [version policy](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/blob/main/docs/version-policy.md).
- [x] **Linting and type checking** pass per `make format` and `make typecheck`.
- [x] **PR title** is fit for the [release changelog](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai/releases).

### Pre-Merge Checklist

- [x] New **tests** for any fix or new behavior, maintaining 100% coverage.
- [x] Updated **documentation** for new features and behaviors, including docstrings for API docs.

### Summary
- Boundary detection now relies on (`run_id`) instead of list-length deltas.

### Behavior Changes

- `new_messages()` starts at the first message where `run_id == state.run_id`.
- For resume-without-prompt runs, boundary detection prefers the reused request:
  - object identity match first,
  - structural match second (`parts`, `timestamp`, `instructions`, `metadata`; intentionally excludes `run_id`),
  - run_id-based detection fallback last.
- After history processing, if the final `ModelRequest` has no `run_id`, we set it to the current run’s `run_id` (except the resumed-tail path where existing semantics are preserved).
- If no current-run `run_id` is found, boundary falls back to `len(messages)` (so only messages appended afterward are considered new).

### Known Edge Cases

- Messages injected before the first current-run `run_id` and without `run_id` are excluded from `new_messages()`.
- Reordered historical messages moved after the computed boundary are treated as new.
- If a processor rewrites all `run_id`s away from the current run, `new_messages()` may effectively include only messages appended after processing (for example, only the response).

comment.body: @DouweM I have updated the PR description
